heading: Water management for rural areas
label: 2204c Water management for rural areas

children.text: conduct research and demonstration projects;
provide technical assistance and extension services;
make grants, loans, and loan guarantees; and

provide other forms of assistance, for the purpose of helping rural areas make better and more efficient use of water resources and to alleviate problems arising in such areas from droughts or lack of water.

children.heading: Activities
children.label: b Activities
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to provide assistance under this section for the promotion or establishment of irrigation, watersheds, and other water management and drought management activities, including water transmission, application, and activation.
should address the general, special, and unique problems of water management existing in rural areas;
may take action independently or in cooperation with Federal, State, public, or private entities and agencies; and

children.label: A
children.text: cooperatives, public or private organizations, confederations, authorities, or other entities (including such entities that may be organized under multiple State agreements or compacts and entities created under State law) to carry out projects authorized under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: water, watershed, and sewer authorities, rural electric cooperatives, Federal agencies, and other State or local governments or agencies.
The Secretary shall issue regulations to carry out this section.

children.label: A
children.text: specify the terms and conditions that the entities described in subsections (a) and (c) must meet in order to participate in programs carried out under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: establish a procedure under which entities described in subsections (a) and (c) may apply for assistance under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: foster cooperation between such entities and other Federal, State, or local agencies for the purposes of carrying out the provisions of this section.
a land grant university established under the Act of (known as the “First Morrill Act”; , chapter 130; et seq.);July 2, 186212 Stat. 5037 U.S.C. 301
a land grant university established under the Act of (known as the “Second Morrill Act”; , chapter 841; et seq.);August 30, 189026 Stat. 4197 U.S.C. 321
the Tuskegee Institute; and
any other support research organization.
There are authorized to be appropriated each fiscal year such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
The Secretary is authorized to accept funds from non-Federal sources to carry out the activities authorized by this section.

children.heading: No waivers
children.label: g No waivers
children.text: Nothing in this section shall authorize the waiver of a cost-share requirement under a program established under any other provision of law.
